questions.question:
A circular sheet of paper is folded along the lines in the directions shown. The paper, after being punched in the final folded state as shown and unfolded in the reverse order of folding, will look like _______.

A. [Circle with two rectangular holes side by side in the centre and a rectangular hole at top centre]
B. [Circle with two rectangular holes in the upper portion arranged horizontally]
C. [Circle with two rectangular holes side by side in the centre and a rectangular hole at top centre — mirror variant]
D. [Circle with one tall rectangular hole in the centre]
questions.answer: A. [Circle with two rectangular holes side by side in the centre and a rectangular hole at top centre]
questions.to_find: appearance of the circular paper after punching and unfolding in reverse fold order

understand.plain: A circular sheet is folded in two steps along the shown lines. After folding, holes are punched through all layers simultaneously. When the paper is unfolded in the reverse order the punch marks appear mirrored across each fold line. The key skill is tracking each hole back through each unfolding step to place all copies correctly on the original circle.

keywords.explain: The line along which the paper is creased; it acts as the axis of reflection for hole positions during unfolding.
keywords.example: Folding left half onto right half creates a vertical fold line; a hole at (x, y) maps to (-x, y) when unfolded.

keywords.explain: A punch made while layers are stacked creates a hole in every layer at the same relative position, so unfolding reveals multiple holes.
keywords.example: One punch through two stacked layers yields two holes after unfolding.

keywords.explain: Unfolding is done in the opposite order of folding; the last fold is undone first, then the first fold, reproducing the full symmetric pattern.
keywords.example: Fold A then fold B => punch => undo B => undo A gives holes placed symmetrically about both fold axes.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 520 180' width='520' height='180'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect width='520' height='180' fill='var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8)' rx='10'/><circle cx='70' cy='90' r='55' fill='none' stroke='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='70' y1='35' x2='70' y2='145' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='5,4'/><text x='70' y='170' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>Step 1: fold left</text><text x='190' y='90' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>-></text><circle cx='310' cy='90' r='55' fill='none' stroke='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='310' y1='35' x2='310' y2='145' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='5,4'/><line x1='255' y1='90' x2='365' y2='90' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='5,4'/><text x='310' y='170' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>Step 2: fold top</text><text x='420' y='90' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>-></text><circle cx='470' cy='90' r='40' fill='none' stroke='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='458' y='82' width='10' height='8' fill='var(--no,#D85A30)' rx='1'/><text x='470' y='148' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>Punch here</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Diagram showing the circular sheet being folded twice then punched, with fold axes marked as dashed lines.

given.aim: Identify the unfolded circular sheet showing all punch holes after reverse unfolding

terms.meaning: A disc-shaped piece of paper that is the base for folding
terms.example: The paper starts as a flat circle before any folding is applied.
terms.connects: All holes must lie within the boundary of this circle after unfolding.

terms.meaning: The sheet is folded twice in the directions shown — once along one axis, then again along a second axis
terms.example: First fold along vertical centre: left half folds onto right. Second fold along horizontal centre: top half folds onto bottom.
terms.connects: Each fold doubles the number of copies a single punch makes when unfolded.

terms.meaning: A rectangular hole is made through all four stacked layers at once after both folds
terms.example: One punch through 4 layers produces 4 symmetric holes on the full sheet.
terms.connects: The final pattern is obtained by reflecting the punch position back through each fold.

terms.meaning: Undo fold 2 first, then fold 1, to place all reflected copies correctly
terms.example: Unfold horizontal fold => 2 holes. Then unfold vertical fold => 4 holes total.
terms.connects: Determines the final symmetric positions of holes on the unfolded circle.

given.plan: Track the punch location through each unfolding step by reflecting it across each fold axis in reverse order. Count total holes and verify they match answer option A.

steps.title: Identify fold axes and punch position
steps.apply: From the figure: Fold 1 is along the vertical centre line (left half onto right). Fold 2 is along the horizontal centre line (top half onto bottom). The punch after both folds is a small rectangle near the centre of the folded (quarter) sheet — this corresponds to a position in the top-right quadrant of the original circle.
steps.note: Reading the folding arrows in the figure carefully is essential; the direction of folding determines which quadrant the punch maps back to.

steps.title: Undo Fold 2 (horizontal): reflect punch across horizontal axis
steps.apply: The single punch in the top-right of the folded sheet, when the horizontal fold is undone, creates a copy reflected below the horizontal axis. We now have 2 hole positions: one in the upper centre area and one in the lower centre area of the right half.
steps.note: After undoing only the second fold, the sheet is still halved (only right half visible).

steps.title: Undo Fold 1 (vertical): reflect both holes across vertical axis
steps.apply: Each of the 2 holes gets a mirror copy on the left side of the circle. This yields 4 holes in total: two side-by-side rectangles in the horizontal centre band and one rectangle at the top centre. The arrangement matches option A: two rectangular holes side by side at centre and one rectangular hole at top.
steps.note: The exact positions depend on where the punch was placed in the folded state — here the punch was off-centre toward the edge, producing the observed pattern.

steps.title: Match with answer options
steps.apply: With 2 folds, 1 punch yields up to 4 holes (2^2 = 4). Option A shows the correct symmetric arrangement of holes within the circular boundary matching the described fold-punch-unfold sequence.
steps.note: Options B, C, D either have wrong positions or wrong number of holes.

solution.result: The unfolded circular sheet looks like option A — with two rectangular holes side by side in the central area and one rectangular hole near the top, all symmetric about the fold axes.

questions.formula_note: Paper folding uses reflection geometry: each hole punched through stacked layers maps to mirror copies across each fold axis when the sheet is unfolded in reverse order.

questions.question:
A transparent square sheet shown above is folded along the dotted line. The folded sheet will look like ___________.

A. [Tall rectangle with leaf/bird shape visible on right portion]
B. [Tall rectangle with leaf/bird shape visible on left portion, slightly tilted]
C. [Tall rectangle with leaf/bird shape visible on right, larger]
D. [Tall rectangle with leaf/bird shape on right, different orientation]
questions.answer: B. [Tall rectangle with leaf/bird shape visible on left portion, slightly tilted]
questions.to_find: appearance of the transparent square sheet after folding along the dotted diagonal-like line

understand.plain: A transparent (see-through) square sheet has a decorative figure printed on it. The sheet is folded along the dotted line shown. Because the sheet is transparent, after folding both the original figure AND its mirror image are visible simultaneously through the overlapping layers. The fold line determines where the crease is and which part of the figure ends up on top.

keywords.meaning: A see-through material so that images on both layers are visible when folded — the image from the back layer shows through the front layer.
keywords.example: A transparency film folded in half shows writing from both halves overlapping.

keywords.meaning: The line marked with dots along which the fold is made; it is the axis of reflection.
keywords.example: A vertical dotted line in the centre of a square means the right half folds onto the left.

keywords.meaning: After folding a square along a line, the outline of the visible region changes to a rectangle or triangle depending on fold direction.
keywords.example: Folding a square along its vertical midline gives a rectangle half as wide; folding along a diagonal gives a triangle.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 480 200' width='480' height='200'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect width='480' height='200' fill='var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8)' rx='10'/><rect x='20' y='30' width='120' height='120' fill='none' stroke='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='80' y1='30' x2='80' y2='150' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='6,4'/><path d='M 55 60 Q 70 50 75 75 Q 80 90 65 110' fill='none' stroke='var(--ok,#1D9E75)' stroke-width='2'/><text x='80' y='178' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>Fold along dashed line</text><text x='185' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>-></text><rect x='230' y='30' width='60' height='120' fill='none' stroke='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M 245 60 Q 255 50 258 75 Q 262 90 250 110' fill='none' stroke='var(--ok,#1D9E75)' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M 275 60 Q 265 50 262 75 Q 258 90 270 110' fill='none' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='3,2'/><text x='260' y='178' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>Folded: both layers visible</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Square sheet with decorative leaf shape folded along vertical dotted midline, producing a tall rectangle showing both original and reflected leaf patterns through transparent layers.

given.aim: Determine the visual appearance of the folded transparent square sheet

terms.meaning: A square sheet has equal width and height; folding along a vertical or near-vertical line creates a taller-than-wide rectangle
terms.example: A 10 cm x 10 cm square folded vertically in half becomes a 5 cm x 10 cm rectangle.
terms.connects: The outline of the answer options should be a tall rectangle (portrait orientation), which narrows down the choices.

terms.term: fold direction — right side folds onto left
terms.meaning: The dotted line is positioned such that folding brings the right half of the square over the left half
terms.example: The printed figure (leaf/bird shape) in the right half folds over to the left side, so its mirror image appears on the left side of the rectangle.
terms.connects: The figure in the left half of the result comes from the mirror of the right-half figure due to folding.

terms.meaning: Both layers are visible simultaneously; the original figure on the bottom layer and its mirror on the top layer overlap, creating a combined silhouette
terms.example: If the left half was blank and the right half had a leaf, after folding the result shows the leaf on the left (folded-over layer visible through the sheet).
terms.connects: This is why the answer shows a combined or reflected pattern rather than just half the original.

terms.term: position and orientation of figure in answer
terms.meaning: After folding, the figure (leaf/bird shape from the original) appears on the left side of the resulting rectangle, oriented as a mirror reflection of the original
terms.example: Option B shows the figure on the left portion of the tall rectangle, which matches the folded-right-over-left orientation.
terms.connects: Matching the position (left vs right) and tilt of the figure identifies the correct option.

given.plan: Determine the fold axis from the dotted line in the figure, track where the printed shape moves after folding, account for transparency showing both layers, and match the result to the answer options.

steps.title: Identify fold axis and direction
steps.apply: From the figure above the question, the dotted fold line runs roughly vertically (slightly diagonal), and the fold direction arrow indicates the right portion folds over onto the left portion. The square sheet thus becomes a tall rectangle after folding.
steps.note: The fold axis being slightly off-vertical introduces a slight tilt to the final shape, consistent with the answer options showing near-vertical rectangles.

steps.title: Track the printed figure through the fold
steps.apply: The original figure (leaf/bird-like curves) is on the right half of the square. After the right half folds over the left half, this figure ends up on the left side of the resulting rectangle but as a mirror image. Since the sheet is transparent, the figure from the back layer shows through, appearing on the left portion of the folded rectangle.
steps.note: For a transparent sheet, both layers are visible, so the figure appears on whichever side it now occupies.

steps.title: Determine outline of folded sheet
steps.formula_raw: folded_outline = overlap of original and reflected halves
steps.apply: Folding the right half over the left produces a tall rectangle (height equals original square side, width equals half the original). The boundary is a clean rectangle with dotted outline indicating the folded edge. This matches the tall rectangular outline seen in all answer options.
steps.note: The slight diagonal of the fold line means the rectangle is very slightly parallelogram-shaped, but this is minor.

steps.title: Match figure position and orientation to options
steps.formula_raw: correct_option = option with figure on left side, mirror-reflected orientation
steps.apply: Option B shows the leaf/bird figure on the left-centre of the tall rectangle, slightly tilted — this matches the fold direction (right-over-left) and the transparency showing the folded right-side figure. Options A, C, D show the figure on the right side or in an incorrect orientation relative to the fold.
steps.note: Option B is the answer as per the official key.

solution.result: After folding the transparent square along the dotted line (right half onto left), the folded sheet looks like option B — a tall rectangle with the leaf/bird-shaped figure visible on the left portion in mirror-reflected orientation.

questions.formula_note: For transparent sheet folding, the folded image appears as a mirror reflection about the fold axis, and since both layers are visible, the combined figure is seen in the resulting folded shape.

formulas.name: Paper Fold Reflection Rule
formulas.plain: Each punch/figure position reflects across the fold axis when unfolded; n folds create up to 2^n copies
